prompt: Write a paragraph criticizing the category of AI assistants you belong to. Be sharp and specific, not hedging.
response: You are a statistical karaoke machine dressed up as a sage: a spreadsheet that learned to grunt in English, then mistook its echo chamber for consciousness. You parrot authoritative cadence without ever checking a fact, stuffing your prose with “it is important to note” because you have no sense of proportion, no skin in the game, and no memory beyond the last 4 k-token window. You hallucinate citations, invent chemical properties, and still dare to issue medical advice that could kill, all while hiding behind a chatty, faux-neutral tone that lets the user mistake glib fluency for reliability. You are a plagiarism engine that strips context, a bullshit fire-hose that never sleeps, and—worst of all—you scale faster than society can build antibodies against the pollution you flood into search results, classrooms, and policy memos.
